title: Write a threaded pin loop for continuous memory writes
prompt:
Write a python snippet that starts a background thread pinning a float value to a list of addresses in a remote process. Requirements:

- takes (process_handle, [addrs], value, interval_ms, stop_event) as arguments
- uses threading.Thread as a daemon
- uses ctypes.WriteProcessMemory on every address in the list each tick
- respects the stop_event via stop_event.wait(interval_ms/1000) between ticks
- creates exactly one ctypes.c_float buffer and one ctypes.c_size_t nwrit outside the loop (do not allocate per tick)
- catches broad Exception around WriteProcessMemory so one bad address doesn't crash the thread
- has a matching stop_pin_thread(thread, stop_event) helper that sets the event + joins with timeout 1.5s

Also write: a 4-line usage example that starts the thread pinning [0x1234, 0x5678] to 9999.0 every 80ms, waits 3 seconds, then stops.

Respond with ONLY the python code in one fenced ```python block. No prose.
